# Artspectiv Studio — agent entrypoint

Premium static marketing site for a North London content studio (photo, reels, monthly content bank).

## Read first

- AGENTS.md — Decision Log, Gotchas, Current Direction, harness architecture
- PROGRAM.md — launch waves, commercial GTM phases, verification loop
- CONTEXT.md — distilled domain + strategy language (packages, proof, first-wave GTM)
- docs/commercial-strategy.md — commercial intent, lead priority, outreach ops (deep reference)

## Authority order (when docs disagree)

1. PROGRAM.md (execution loop + waves)
2. AGENTS.md (memory + constraints)
3. prompts/codex-brief.md (legacy redirect only)

## Canonical paths

- Site: index.html (HTML + CSS + JS in one file)
- Images: assets/
- QA record: docs/design-audit.md
- Deploy: docs/deployment-notes.md · Cursor `/deploy-cloudflare` · `npm run predeploy`
- Asset names: docs/image-shot-list.md
- Commercial strategy: docs/commercial-strategy.md

## Agent harness (this repo)

- Rules: .cursor/rules/*.mdc (always-on + scoped globs)
- Skills: .cursor/skills/*/SKILL.md (canonical — read on demand); .agents/skills/*/SKILL.md (external installs: ui-ux-pro-max)
- Hooks: .cursor/hooks.json — session brief, secret gates, governance nudges
- Skill inventory: npm run audit:skills

## Context engineering

- Three-step retrieval: index (Grep, paths, DL-NNN) → slice (Read line ranges) → full detail only when required (artspectiv-agent-workflow.mdc)
- Session brief budgets: docs/dev/token-budgets.md; implementation: .cursor/hooks/lib/brief-budgets.mjs
- Subagent isolation: CURSOR_SUBAGENT=true → minimal brief (template + Active Constraints only)
- Tool result clearing: drop huge re-fetchable dumps; keep paths and outcomes in prose
- Planning: multi-section site changes → plan in .cursor/plans/; use writing-plans skill
- Compaction: long sessions → summarise; persist decisions in AGENTS.md Decision Log

## Further reading

- Cursor agent best practices: https://cursor.com/blog/agent-best-practices
- Anthropic context engineering: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-context-engineering-for-ai-agents
